medications.indication_rationale: Empirical guideline treatment for uncomplicated cystitis; source used nitrofurantoin 100mg twice daily for 5 days. Confirm pregnancy status and renal function/allergies (avoid nitrofurantoin at term/eGFR<45; avoid trimethoprim in early pregnancy).
medications.dose_route_frequency: nitrofurantoin 100mg (modified-release) twice daily for 5 days, OR trimethoprim 300mg daily for 3 days (as per source note / guideline)

medications.indication_rationale: May ease stinging (note: do not co-administer with nitrofurantoin as alkalinisation reduces its efficacy); source gave an alkaliniser.
Do not prescribe without screening upper-tract/systemic features and confirming pregnancy status/drug suitability.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Increase fluid intake to flush the bladder; complete the full antibiotic course; wipe front-to-back and void after intercourse to reduce recurrence.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Hydration and hygiene support recovery and reduce recurrence.

patient_education_points.point: This is a bladder infection (cystitis). A short course of antibiotics clears it — the burning usually eases within a day and the frequency settles over 2–3 days. Drink plenty of fluids and finish the whole course.

Take the antibiotic as prescribed (and if it's nitrofurantoin, avoid urinary alkalinisers, which reduce its effect). Wipe front-to-back and empty your bladder after sex to help prevent it coming back.

Go to ED / see a doctor urgently if you develop a fever or shakes, pain in your back or side (kidney area), nausea/vomiting, or you feel very unwell — that suggests the infection has reached the kidneys. Also check in if you might be pregnant, or if symptoms don't improve within 48 hours.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Response; urine culture if failed/recurrent; confirm pregnancy status/drug suitability.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Fever/loin pain/systemic features — ED; possible pregnancy — GP for a suitable antibiotic.

must_include_items: Screen pyelonephritis/systemic features and complicating factors (pregnancy!) before empirical antibiotics
Pyelonephritis safety-net (fever, loin pain, vomiting → ED)
Confirm pregnancy status / choose a pregnancy-safe antibiotic
acceptable_alternatives: Nitrofurantoin or trimethoprim per local guidance; urine culture if recurrent/failed/complicated
errors_of_omission: Not checking pregnancy status / complicating factors
No advice on completing the course / fluids
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires a pyelonephritis/complicating-factor (incl. pregnancy) screen, a guideline short-course antibiotic, and a pyelonephritis safety-net.
